title: Caramelized Jamaican Rice Curry
description: A herbal, chewy lazy‑Sunday Jamaican dish featuring lemongrass and caramelized rice, perfect for a low‑calorie friendly meal.

ingredients.name: Long‑grain white rice
ingredients.notes: Rinse under cold water until clear.

ingredients.name: Lemongrass stalks
ingredients.amount: 2 stalks, bruise and cut into 2‑inch pieces

instructions: Rinse the rice under cold water until clear. Drain.

In a medium saucepan, combine rinsed rice, water, lemongrass, and a pinch of salt. Bring to a boil, reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 15 minutes or until water is absorbed. Remove from heat, fluff, and set aside.

While the rice cooks, heat a large skillet over medium heat. Add a splash of water or oil, then sauté diced onion, minced garlic, grated ginger, and sliced scallions until the onions soften and begin to caramelize, about 5–6 minutes.
Stir in the brown sugar and cook for another 2 minutes, letting it melt and coat the vegetables for a light caramel.
Add the cooked rice to the skillet, breaking up any clumps. Stir to combine, ensuring the rice is coated with the caramelized mixture.
Pour in the coconut milk, lime juice, and chopped thyme. Stir, bring to a gentle simmer, and let the flavors meld for 5 minutes. Taste and adjust seasoning with salt and pepper.
Remove from heat, cover, and let the curry sit for 5 minutes to allow the rice to absorb the liquid and become chewy.
Fluff the rice again, serve hot, and optionally garnish with a sprinkle of fresh scallion or cilantro.
